model.label: DeepSeek V3.2
model.description: DeepSeek • General-purpose V-series model optimized for reasoning, coding, and efficient inference.

pick.rationale: Wisconsin's elite top-10 offense, built against a rigorous Big Ten schedule, should overwhelm High Point's vulnerable defense. The Badgers are expected to be at full strength with Nolan Winter's return, providing additional frontcourt depth and scoring. While High Point's 30-4 record is impressive, their weaker competition and defensive limitations make a 12-seed upset unlikely in this matchup.

reasoningStep.title: Efficiency and Health Advantage Favor Wisconsin
reasoningStep.summary: Wisconsin's significant edge in adjusted efficiency metrics, especially on offense, combined with a positive injury update and high-major experience, outweighs High Point's impressive win total from a weaker conference.
evidence: Torvik ratings: Wisconsin 21 (Off: 8, Def: 48), High Point 93 (Off: 73, Def: 148). KenPom: Wisconsin 22, High Point 92. Wisconsin's key forward Nolan Winter is expected back for the tournament. High Point's 30-4 record came against Big South competition, while Wisconsin went 24-10 in the Big Ten.

result.text: Wisconsin vs. High Point: NCAA Tournament Predictions & Preview | BettingPros to prevent flash of incorrect theme on page load --> Skip to main content Skip to navigation Skip to footer Click to go to homepage Picks Odds Tools Research Leaders Click to expand More menu Home Games Picks Upgrade Menu Click to Find a bet ... Contest Click to expand picks panel Click to open Sharp AI Wisconsin vs. High Point: NCAA Tournament Predictions & Preview by BettingPros Staff | @BettingPros | Mar 15, 2026 by BettingPros Staff | @BettingPros | Mar 15, 2026 The 2026 NCAA Tournament is officially here! The March Madness Bracket is set, and first-round NCAA Tournament matchups are in place. It’s time to make your picks and predictions for the first round of the 2026 NCAA Tournament! We’re here to help as we’ll have picks and predictions for each of the first round 2026 NCAA Tournament games. Here are our NCAA Tournament predictions and preview for Wisconsin vs. High Point. NCAA Tournament Odds NCAA Tournament Pick’Em Contest NCAA Tournament March Madness Bracket Optimizer NCAA Tournament Betting Trends 2026 NCAA Tournament Predictions & Preview: Wisconsin vs. High Point Here are the odds for this opening-round matchup of the 2026 NCAA Tournament. Let’s dive into our preview and predictions for this NCAA Tournament matchup. Wisconsin 2026 NCAA Tournament Preview Greg Gard has built one of the more fascinating two-sided profiles in the field. On offense, Wisconsin plays at a Final Four level. Nick Boyd has been one of the most consistent players in a loaded conference all year, earning All-Big Ten second-team honors. John Blackwell as the second scorer gives the Badgers two engines who can get hot simultaneously, as they showed in a monster road win at Purdue, where the two combined for 48 points, and in a win over Illinois in the second round of the conference tourney, where they combined for 69 points. Mix in Austin Rapp and Braeden Carrington's ability to microwave from deep, along with the third-lowest team turnover percentage in the nation, and it adds up to one of the best offenses in D-I hoops. This is a team that went 7-3 ATS as an underdog this year. The Badgers live and die by the three, with over 42% of their points coming from deep, fourth-most in the nation, which can be good and bad. When Wisconsin shot under 33.3% from deep this year, they went 3-8. They went 21-2 in all other games, including wins over Purdue, Michigan, Illinois, and Michigan State. The problem is the defense, which ranks 50th in adjusted efficiency. Wisconsin was been beaten handily in games where the offense didn’t show up early in the season. Against non-con opponents, the Badgers went 8-3 and dropped to 49th overall before figuring out their offense. Another concern entering the tourney is Nolan Winter’s ankle injury, which has left him day-to-day. Per Hoop Explorer, Winter has one of the biggest impacts on team efficiency in all of

result.text: Wisconsin's Greg Gard has injury update on Nolan Winter, Jack Janicki Skip to main content Home Olympics NFL NCAAB NCAAW NBA Women's Sports NCAAF MLB WNBA Sports Betting Big Ten Sports SEC Sports Boxing Coaches Salaries Draft Hub Fantasy Football For The Win Formula 1 Golfweek High School Sports High School Sports Awards IndyCar MMA NASCAR National Signing Day NHL Soccer Sports Business Sports Pulse Sports Seriously Tennis WWE Youth Sports Outdoors Fantasy Bet With the People Clear Play Arizona Sports Indiana Sports Michigan Sports Ohio Sports Tennessee Sports Wisconsin Teams For Subscribers Wisconsin coach Greg Gard gives injury updates on Nolan Winter, Jack Janicki John Steppe Milwaukee Journal Sentinel Show Caption Hide Caption Wisconsin coach Greg Gard comments on 'tremendous' game at Purdue Greg Gard took pride in the 'character of the guys in that locker room' as the Badgers won despite Nolan Winter and Jack Janicki's injury absences. Wisconsin forward Nolan Winter is expected to return in time for the NCAA Tournament after a lower-body injury. The team is not rushing Winter's return, but he is making good progress. Guard Jack Janicki's return from wrist surgery is a possibility for the NCAA Tournament. MADISON – Wisconsin forward Nolan Winter continues officially to be "day-to-day" with his lower-body injury, but his return appears to be closer on the horizon. "He's trying to be ready for this week and definitely will be ready by the NCAA Tournament," Wisconsin coach Greg Gard said. "We're not rushing him back and doing anything crazy, knowing he'll be ready to rock by the next week for sure." Gard also expressed optimism about Winter's recovery after the team's March 7 win over Purdue, saying that UW's medical staff has been "very satisfied with where he’s at in terms of his progress each day." More: Where Wisconsin is ranked in AP, coaches polls after upsetting Purdue Winter suffered the injury in the second half of Wisconsin's 33-point win over Maryland on March 4. He was helped off the court while appearing to not put any weight on his left side. Winter has averaged 13.3 points and 8.6 rebounds and led a frontcourt that does not necessarily have an abundance of depth. Wisconsin guard Jack Janicki's timeline to return from wrist surgery does not appear to be as definitive. Janicki suffered the injury in the Badgers ' Feb. 17 loss to Ohio State and had a procedure on it later that week. He has been wearing a brace on his left wrist. "He had another evaluation today," Gard said. "It looks really good. I think he meets with his doctor again, I think, early next week. So potentially by the NCAA Tournament, there could be a possibility with him there, too." The Badgers have used a smaller rotation in the absence of Winter and Janicki, as only six players were on the floor for 10-plus minutes in the March 7 win over Purdue. UW's first game in the Big Ten Tournament will be March 12 against the winner of 12th-seeded Washington and 13th-seeded
